text.title: Help and Documentation Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Help and Documentation. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Help content is task-oriented ('how do I export a report'), not feature-oriented ('the export module').
rule.rationale: Feature-oriented docs do not match user search queries.

rule.statement: Inline help (tooltip, drawer, link) appears at the point of need, not only in a separate knowledge base.
rule.rationale: Help confined to a help centre is never read in flow.

rule.statement: Knowledge base supports search with synonym handling and analytics on no-result queries.
rule.rationale: Search without synonyms misses common queries.

rule.statement: Each article has a thumbs-up/down or 'was this helpful' signal; signals are reviewed monthly.
rule.rationale: Without a feedback loop articles rot silently.

rule.statement: Error messages link to the relevant help article when one exists.
rule.rationale: Errors and help disconnected force users to copy-paste into search.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-help-documentation.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
